item.title: AI Research Brief: 2026-07-20
item.description: Weekly issue covering high-signal AI research and engineering work published or newly updated between July 13 and July 20, 2026, with emphasis on automated red-teaming, coding-agent supply-chain risk, cost-aware security agents, agentic search, GUI supervision, recommender feedback loops, LLM consistency, diffusion language models, and robot context scaling.

item.title: AI Research Brief: 2026-07-17
item.description: Weekly issue covering high-signal AI research and engineering work published or newly updated between July 10 and July 17, 2026, with emphasis on agent abstention, isolation, permissions, cross-device agents, adaptive memory, long-horizon RL, decentralized LLM training, vector search, and recommender systems.

item.title: AI Research Brief: 2026-07-15
item.description: Weekly issue covering high-signal AI research and engineering work published or newly updated between July 8 and July 15, 2026, with emphasis on agent red-teaming, compositional safety failures, benchmark validity, visual tool use, LLM-as-judge bias, GraphRAG, robotics access, model values, and diversity-aware recommendation.

item.title: AI Research Brief: 2026-07-13
item.description: Weekly issue covering high-signal AI research and engineering work published or newly updated between July 6 and July 13, 2026, with emphasis on interpretable model workspaces, controllable dual-use knowledge, agent failure diagnosis, AI-native data workflows, recommender systems, multimodal memory, and cost-aware LLM serving.

item.title: AI Research Brief: 2026-07-10
item.description: Weekly issue covering high-signal AI research and engineering work published or newly updated between July 3 and July 10, 2026, with emphasis on realistic agent benchmarks, long-horizon memory, tool-use diagnostics, web and software agents, agentic recommender systems, privacy, execution integrity, and efficient evaluation.

item.title: AI Research Brief: 2026-06-10
item.description: Weekly issue covering high-signal AI research and engineering work published or newly updated between June 3 and June 10, 2026, with emphasis on evaluation reporting, rubric-driven training, personalized agents, agent RL infrastructure, memory systems, scientific coding agents, and LLM security.

item.title: AI Research Brief: 2026-06-08
item.description: Weekly issue covering high-signal AI research and engineering work published or newly updated between June 1 and June 8, 2026, with emphasis on agent memory, agentic RAG, long-horizon agent training, sparse-attention serving, local multimodal models, code adaptation, theorem proving, and AI-for-science evaluation.

item.title: AI Research Brief: 2026-06-05
item.description: Weekly issue covering high-signal AI research and engineering work published or newly surfaced between May 29 and June 5, 2026, with emphasis on search agents, deep-research auditing, agent harnesses, skill generation, personalization, reward hacking, RAG faithfulness, and inference efficiency.

item.title: AI Research Brief: 2026-06-03
item.description: Weekly issue covering high-signal AI research and engineering work published or updated between May 27 and June 3, 2026, with emphasis on production agent monitoring, MCP privacy, skill attacks, continual-learning agents, web-browsing benchmarks, safety alignment, retrieval routing, recommender robustness, and inference efficiency.

item.title: AI Research Brief: 2026-06-01
item.description: Weekly issue covering high-signal AI research and engineering work published or updated between May 25 and June 1, 2026, with emphasis on agent auditing, verifier training, long-horizon memory, RAG systems, code review automation, recommender audits, and AI safety governance.

item.title: AI Research Brief: 2026-05-29
item.description: Weekly issue covering high-signal AI research and engineering work published or updated between May 22 and May 29, 2026, with emphasis on agent evaluation, memory systems, mobile and web agents, verifier training, tool-use safety, PEFT, recommender systems, and code-generation bias.

item.title: AI Research Brief: 2026-05-27
item.description: Weekly issue covering high-signal AI research and engineering work published or updated between May 20 and May 27, 2026, with emphasis on mobile and computer-use agents, agent harnesses, benchmark auditing, long-term memory, scientific reasoning environments, and recommender-system reranking.

item.title: AI Research Brief: 2026-05-25
item.description: Weekly issue covering high-signal AI research and engineering work published or updated between May 18 and May 25, 2026, with emphasis on AI-for-research milestones, agent infrastructure, agent evaluation, post-training, long-context efficiency, and coding-agent behavior.

item.title: AI Research Brief: 2026-05-22
item.description: Weekly issue covering high-signal AI-for-science agents, web and research agents, RLVR training, agent safety, retrieval, and recommender-system work published or updated between May 15 and May 22, 2026.

item.title: AI Research Brief: 2026-05-20
item.description: Weekly issue covering strong agent infrastructure, long-context efficiency, retrieval, recommender, alignment, and safety work published or updated between May 13 and May 20, 2026.

item.title: AI Research Brief: 2026-05-18
item.description: Weekly issue covering strong agent, memory, retrieval, safety, recommender, and model-efficiency work published or updated between May 11 and May 18, 2026.

item.title: AI Research Brief: 2026-05-16
item.description: First verified issue covering recent agent, multimodal, interaction, and privacy research and technical releases published or updated between May 6 and May 15, 2026.
